mnemonics: SVD: Some Vectors Decompose - Remember that Singular Value Decomposition breaks a matrix into three simpler matrices that represent rotation, scaling, and another rotation
keyPoints: Optimization in linear algebra often involves finding solutions that minimize error or maximize fit
Techniques like least squares solve Ax=b by minimizing the squared residual norm ||b - Ax||²
Eigenvalue problems help find directions of maximum variance or stability in systems
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check if a matrix is full rank before applying optimization techniques
Confusing the properties of eigenvectors for different matrices (symmetric vs non-symmetric)
Misapplying the normal equations AᵀAx = Aᵀb without considering numerical stability issues
